journal_entry.description: Discounted 90-day non-interest-bearing note at bank, 15% annual discount rate, 40 days remaining
journal_entry.rationale: Ford discounts the note 50 days after issue, leaving 40 days to maturity. Bank discount = $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Net proceeds = $100,000 - $1,643.84 = $98,356.16. Since management has determined this qualifies as derecognition under IFRS 9 (transfer of risks and rewards), the note receivable is removed from the books. The difference between the note's carrying value ($100,000) and net proceeds ($98,356.16) is recognized as a loss on discounting ($1,643.84).

output_debit_classifier.reason: Note discounting with derecognition: cash increases (net proceeds), loss on discounting recognized (expense), notes receivable decreases. Bank discount calculated as $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84 for the remaining 40 days to maturity.
